 distribution if no performance data is availableUUPS upgradeable with AccessControlRLS is an ERC-20 token; USDr is ERC-20 stablecoin","errors":{"AccessControlBadConfirmation()":[{"details":"The caller of a function is not the expected one. This account bears the admin role (for the granted role). Expected in cases where the role was granted using the internal {AccessControl-_grantRole}."},"RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)"},"Upgraded(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the implementation is upgraded."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"claimRewards(address)":{"details":"Only the validator themselves can claim their rewards"},"constructor":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow":"constructor"},"distributeRewards()":{"details":"Distributes all pending rewards in a single call.      Uses performance weights (stake \u00d7 headerCount) if available, falls back to pure stake.      Fetches each validator's own-stake and delegated-stake exactly once (used for both      APY top-up calculation and the validator/pool reward split)."},"getPendingRewards(address)":{"params":{"validatorAddress":"The validator's address"},"returns":{"_0":"The pending reward amount for the validator"}},"getRewardRecipient(address)":{"details":"Returns the validator address itself if no custom recipient is set"},"getRoleAdmin(bytes32)":{"details":"Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}."},"grantRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event."},"hasRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Returns `true` if `account` has been granted `role`."},"proxiableUUID()":{"details":"Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"receiveRewards(uint256)":{"details":"Called by FeeAggregator after swapping USDr to RLSFeeAggregator must transfer RLS tokens before calling this","params":{"amount":"The amount of RLS tokens received"}},"recoverTokens(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Cannot recover RLS that is pending distribution or unclaimed"},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"setAccumulator(address)":{"params":{"newAccumulator":"The new accumulator address"}},"setDelegationPool(address)":{"params":{"newPool":"The new DelegationPool address"}},"setFeeAggregator(address)":{"params":{"newAggregator":"The new FeeAggregator address"}},"setRewardRecipient(address)":{"params":{"recipient":"The address to receive rewards (address(0) to reset to self)"}},"setTargetApyBps(uint256)":{"params":{"newApyBps":"The new target APY"}},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas."},"totalPendingRewards()":{"returns":{"_0":"The total RLS balance pending distribution"}},"upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes)":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow-reachable":"delegatecall","details":"Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. claimRewards(address).details: Only the validator themselves can claim their rewards

distributeRewards().details: Distributes all pending rewards in a single call.      Uses performance weights (stake × headerCount) if available, falls back to pure stake.      Fetches each validator's own-stake and delegated-stake exactly once (used for both      APY top-up calculation and the validator/pool reward split).

returns._0: The pending reward amount for the validator

getRewardRecipient(address).details: Returns the validator address itself if no custom recipient is set

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

receiveRewards(uint256).details: Called by FeeAggregator after swapping USDr to RLSFeeAggregator must transfer RLS tokens before calling this

params.amount: The amount of RLS tokens received

recoverTokens(address,address,uint256).details: Cannot recover RLS that is pending distribution or unclaimed

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.recipient: The address to receive rewards (address(0) to reset to self)

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

returns._0: The total RLS balance pending distribution

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

claimRewards(address).notice: Claim pending rewards for a validator

distributeRewards().notice: Distribute all pending rewards to active validators and their delegation pools

getPendingRewards(address).notice: Get pending rewards for a specific validator

getRewardRecipient(address).notice: Get the reward recipient for a validator

receiveRewards(uint256).notice: Receive ERC-20 RLS rewards from FeeAggregator

recoverTokens(address,address,uint256).notice: Emergency function to recover stuck ERC-20 tokens

rlsToken().notice: Get the RLS token address (ERC-20 staking token)

setAccumulator(address).notice: Set the RLS Accumulator address for APY top-ups

setRewardRecipient(address).notice: Set the reward recipient for the calling validator

setTargetApyBps(uint256).notice: Set the target APY in basis points (e.g., 5000 = 50%)

targetApyBps().notice: Get the target APY in basis points

totalPendingRewards().notice: Get total undistributed rewards held by the contract
